name: Open Interpreter
pricing_notes: Open source; bring your LLM API key; OS1 cloud model subscription available

auth.notes: Framework only; configure LLM in environment; OS1 cloud uses account auth

performance.notes: Code execution latency depends on language runtime; LLM calls dominate overall latency

agent_readiness.notes: Local code execution agent: runs Python, JavaScript, shell, and more on user machine. Conversational interface for code execution. Terminal and programmatic Python API. Works with any LLM (OpenAI, Anthropic, local Ollama models). OS mode for computer use (experimental). No sandboxing by default — runs directly on host machine. Better for interactive dev use than production agentic pipelines.

gotchas: No sandbox — code runs directly on host machine; security risk in automated contexts
Not production-safe for untrusted inputs (no isolation layer)
OS mode (computer use) is experimental and unreliable
Development focus shifted toward OS1 commercial product — OSS less maintained
best_when: Interactive local development assistant on developer machines
Data science workflows where LLM + Python execution on local data is needed
Rapid prototyping of agentic code execution concepts
Trusted single-user environments where sandboxing isn't a concern
avoid_when: Multi-tenant or user-facing applications (no sandboxing = dangerous)
Production agentic pipelines (use E2B or Modal for sandboxed execution)
Automated CI/CD contexts without human review
summary: Open Interpreter lets LLMs run code locally on your machine (Python, JS, shell, etc.) through a conversational interface. Python SDK for programmatic use. Works with any LLM. No sandboxing — code runs on host, making it unsafe for automated/multi-tenant contexts. Best as an interactive developer tool for trusted local use. For production sandboxed execution, use E2B or Modal instead.
